description_long: This object is a pair of pliers featuring two elongated, slightly curved handles coated in a dark green material, likely for improved grip and comfort. The handles are connected to a robust black metal head, which houses the pivot mechanism and the jaws. The jaws are short and appear to be flat with a possible cutting edge, suitable for gripping, bending, or cutting wires and small objects. The pivot is a single, fixed joint, allowing the handles to open and close the jaws efficiently. The handles have a smooth, slightly contoured design, providing ergonomic support for the user. No visible branding or logos are present on the exterior surfaces.
description: A pair of pliers with green ergonomic handles and a black metal head, featuring a fixed pivot and flat jaws. The handles are smooth and slightly curved for comfort.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the green handles and the black metal head, highlighting the ergonomic handle shape.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the open jaws and the parallel alignment of the handles, emphasizing the gripping area.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the other side of the handles and head.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the handle ends and the back of the pivot mechanism, with the jaws less visible.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, slightly curved, insulated with smooth green plastic for ergonomic grip.
attribute_jaw_design: Short, flat jaws with a possible cutting edge, suitable for gripping and cutting tasks; lacks serrations or needle-nose features.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Single fixed pivot joint, non-adjustable, standard for basic pliers.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth plastic coating with subtle contouring, no visible ribbing or knurling.
